zh.lifeSummary: 當Brian I Cathan An Duin O'Neil, "High King of Ireland"於大約1187年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 泰龍時，他的父親King Niall of Tyrone13歲，母親Nuala Ní Conchobair22歲。他於大約1249年與Julia O'Donnell在愛爾蘭庫迪 安特里姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1259年登記服兵役。他去世於1260年5月16日在愛爾蘭庫迪 唐堂帕特利可享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Brian I Cathan An Duin O'Neil, "High King of Ireland"于大约1187年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 泰龍时，他的父亲King Niall of Tyrone13岁，母亲Nuala Ní Conchobair22岁。他于大约1249年与Julia O'Donnell在爱尔兰庫迪 安特里姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1259年登记服兵役。他去世于1260年5月16日在爱尔兰庫迪 唐堂帕特利可享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: O'Neil,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Conn, Donal, Liam, Siobhan, Aileen, Eamon, Michael Patrick, Seamus, Sean Patrick, William Sean.

variants.name: O'Neill
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Seamus, Siobhan, Dermot, Liam, Nuala, Bridie, Brigid, Conor, Fintan, John Patrick.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Néill ‘descendant of Niall’, an Irish personal name possibly derived from néal ‘cloud’ (see Neil ).

History: Bearers of this surname mostly claim descent from Niall Noígíallach (Niall of the Nine Hostages), a legendary king of Ireland who flourished in the 5th century. His descendants, the Ui Neill dynasty, dominated Ireland, on and off, from the 6th to the 10th century. The surname itself can be traced with some confidence to one of these descendants, namely Niall Glúndubh mac Aodha (died 919), High King of Ireland from 911 to 919, with a power base in Tyrone, Ulster.

nameMeanings.name: Brian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Irish origin: perhaps from an Old Celtic word meaning ‘high’ or ‘noble’. The name has been perennially popular in Ireland, in particular on account of the fame of Brian Boru (Gaelic Brian Bóroimhe) ( c. 940–1014 ), a warrior who was credited with driving the Vikings from Ireland and who eventually became high king of Ireland. In the Middle Ages it was relatively common in East Anglia, where it was introduced by Breton settlers, and in northern England, where it was introduced by Scandinavians from Ireland. It was quite popular in Yorkshire in the early 16th century, largely because it had long been a family name among the Stapletons, who had Irish connections. They first used it after Sir Gilbert Stapleton married Agnes , the daughter of the great northern baron Sir Brian fitzAlan . In Gaelic Scotland it was at first borne exclusively by members of certain professional families of Irish origin.

variants.name: Bryan
meanings: Variant of   Brian  , influenced by the usual spelling of the associated surname.

nameMeanings.name: Cathan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name from a diminutive of cath ‘battle’.

nameMeanings.name: An
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Duin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: (also Van Duin): topographic name from duin ‘dune’, for someone who came from a hamlet located in the dunes, or a habitational name for someone from any of several places in the Netherlands called Duin.
nickname for a caretaker or warden of a part of the coastal dune landscape, shortened from duinmeier (see Dunmire ).

variants.name: Dunmire
meanings: Irish: Dun, the fort, an, of the, and oir, gold; originally, Dun-an-oir

en.lifeSummary: When Brian I Cathan An Duin O'Neil, "High King of Ireland" was born about 1187, in County Tyrone, Ireland, his father, King Niall of Tyrone, was 13 and his mother, Nuala Ní Conchobair, was 22. He married Julia O'Donnell about 1249, in County Antrim, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He registered for military service in 1259. He died on 16 May 1260, in Downpatrick, County Down, Ireland, at the age of 74.

fr.County Tyrone, Northern Ireland, United: County Tyrone, Irlande du Nord, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Brian I Cathan An Duin O'Neil, "High King of Ireland" est né environ 1187, à County Tyrone, Irlande, son père, King Niall of Tyrone, avait 13 ans et sa mère, Nuala Ní Conchobair, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Julia O'Donnell environ 1249, à County Antrim, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1259. Il est décédé le 16 mai 1260, à Downpatrick, County Down, Irlande, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Brian I Cathan An Duin O'Neil, "High King of Ireland" um 1187 in County Tyrone, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater King Niall of Tyrone 13 und seine Mutter Nuala Ní Conchobair 22. Er hat um 1249 in County Antrim, Irland Julia O'Donnell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er hat sich 1259 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 16. Mai 1260 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Downpatrick, County Down, Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Brian I Cathan An Duin O'Neil, "High King of Ireland" è nato il circa 1187 nel County Tyrone, Irlanda, il suo padre, King Niall of Tyrone, aveva 13 anni e il suo madre, Nuala Ní Conchobair, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Julia O'Donnell circa 1249, nel County Antrim, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1259. Morì il 16 maggio 1260, nel Downpatrick, County Down, Irlanda, all'età di 74 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Brian I Cathan An Duin O'Neil, "High King of Ireland"が約1187年にアイルランドコウントイ トイロンで生まれた時，父King Niall of Tyroneは13歳，母Nuala Ní Conchobairは22歳であった。約1249年にアイルランドコウントイ アントリムでJulia O'Donnellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1259年に兵役登録をした。彼は1260年5月16日にアイルランドコウントイ ダウンダウンプアトリックで74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Brian I Cathan An Duin O'Neil, "High King of Ireland"(이)가 대략 1187년 아일랜드 카운티 타이론에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 King Niall of Tyrone(은)는 13세였고, 그의 어머니 Nuala Ní Conchobair(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1249년 아일랜드 카운티 앤트림에서 Julia O'Donnell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1259년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1260년 5월 16일 아일랜드 카운티 다운 다운팻릭에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Brian I Cathan An Duin O'Neil, "High King of Ireland" nasceu aproximadamente 1187, em County Tyrone, Irlanda, seu pai, King Niall of Tyrone, tinha 13 anos e sua mãe, Nuala Ní Conchobair, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Julia O'Donnell aproximadamente 1249, em County Antrim, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1259. Ele faleceu em 16 de maio de 1260, em Downpatrick, County Down, Irlanda, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Brian I Cathan An Duin O'Neil, "High King of Ireland" родился около 1187 г. в местности коунти тироун, Ирландия, его отцу по имени King Niall of Tyrone было 13 лет, а матери, которую звали Nuala Ní Conchobair, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Julia O'Donnell около 1249 г. в местности коунти антрим, Ирландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. В 1259 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 16 мая 1260 г. в местности доунпатрикк, коунти доун, Ирландия в возрасте 74 лет.
